Speaking Template

A simple frame so you never freeze

Introduction
“Today I'd like to talk about … The reason I chose this is …”

Main points
“Firstly, … Secondly, … Another important point is …”

Example
“For example, in my work at GSI …”

Conclusion
“To sum up, … Thank you for listening.”

Useful connectors
FirstlyIn additionHowever As a resultFor instanceTo sum up

Reference Library

Ready-made language for work. Tap a section to open it.

Grammar Book

Wren & Martin Books I & II (complete), Norman Lewis vocabulary, phrasal verbs & sentence analysis

The complete official text — all 43 chapters of Wren & Martin Book I, straight from the book.

Wren & Martin Book II — sentence structure, direct/indirect speech, and every writing skill: paragraphs, letters, essays, precis, dialogue, comprehension, story writing.

Norman Lewis's Word Power Made Easy — the introduction on building vocabulary, plus several full teaching sessions. (A partial excerpt of this 562-page book — ask if you'd like more sessions added.)

167 phrasal verbs grouped by base verb — tap a group to expand.
